entries.name: evidence
entries.description: A type of information that is used to support an assertion.

entries.name: inference from background scientific knowledge
entries.description: A type of curator inference where conclusions are drawn based on the background scientific knowledge of the curator.

entries.name: direct assay evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental evidence resulting from the direct measurement of some aspect of a biological feature.

entries.name: reconstitution assay evidence
entries.description: A type of direct assay evidence based on reconstructing a biological sample from its disassociated state to its original state.

entries.name: cell fractionation evidence
entries.description: A type of fractionation evidence where sub-cellular components are separated based on their physical properties such as density in a sucrose density gradient.

entries.name: enzymatic activity assay evidence
entries.description: A type of protein assay evidence where the catalytic activity of an enzyme is determined.

entries.name: experimental evidence
entries.description: A type of evidence that is the output of a scientific procedure performed to make a discovery, test a hypothesis, or demonstrate a known fact.

entries.name: immunofluorescence evidence
entries.description: A type of protein detection assay evidence where a fluorescently labeled antibody is used to detect the presence or localization of a biomolecule within a cell.

entries.name: expression pattern evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental evidence that is based on characterization of gene expression.

entries.name: transcript expression evidence
entries.description: A type of expression pattern evidence where abundance of a transcript is analyzed.

entries.name: protein expression evidence
entries.description: A type of expression pattern evidence resulting from protein abundance quantification techniques.

entries.name: genetic interaction evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental phenotypic evidence resulting from the effect that a given gene has on another gene or genes, and the products.

entries.name: functional complementation evidence
entries.description: A type of genetic interaction evidence where a wild-type copy of the gene in question is inserted into a mutant cell to see if it restores the wild-type phenotype in the mutant background.

entries.name: transgenic rescue experiment evidence
entries.description: A type of functional complementation evidence resulting from the introduction of a transgene to prevent, or "rescue" an organism from a condition.

entries.name: mutant phenotype evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental phenotypic evidence in which an observable phenotypic difference results from a change or mutation in DNA.

entries.name: loss-of-function mutant phenotype evidence
entries.description: A type of mutant phenotype evidence where a phenotype is associated with altered gene product which lacks the molecular function of the wild-type gene.

entries.name: ectopic expression evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental phenotypic evidence where a transgenic strain carrying the construct of a promoter cDNA fusion in which a gene of interest is driven by a defined promoter or enhancer is ectopically expressed in the defined pattern to characterize potential cellular properties and functions of a protein of interest.

entries.name: anti-sense experiment evidence
entries.description: A type of mutant phenotype evidence where a phenotype is observed while expressing an anti-sense version of a gene product in a wild-type (for that gene product) background.

entries.name: RNAi evidence
entries.description: A type of mutant phenotype evidence where an RNA construct is introduced into a cell and the expression of the gene bearing its complementary sequence is suppressed.

entries.name: protein inhibition evidence
entries.description: A type of direct assay evidence based on the inhibition of the molecular function of a protein.

entries.name: physical interaction evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental evidence that is based on characterization of an interaction between a gene product and another molecule.

entries.name: co-purification evidence
entries.description: A type of physical interaction evidence where a cellular component subunit is isolated as part of purification of its larger complex.

entries.name: affinity evidence
entries.description: A type of physical interaction evidence that depends on the strength of the interaction between two entities.

entries.name: protein binding evidence
entries.description: A type of affinity evidence resulting from the binding of a molecule to a protein or protein complex.

entries.name: bait-prey hybrid interaction evidence
entries.description: A type of protein binding evidence where proteins of interest (bait and prey) are covalently linked to incomplete fragments of a third protein (reporter) and expressed in vivo, at which time interaction between bait and prey proteins brings reporter fragments in close enough proximity to allow them to reform and become a functional reporter protein.

entries.name: obsolete nucleic acid hybridization evidence
entries.description: OBSOLETE. A type of experimental genomic evidence resulting from a process in which single stranded nucleic acids are allowed to interact so that complexes, or hybrids, are formed by molecules with sufficiently similar, complementary sequences.

entries.name: structural similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of similarity evidence based on structural similarity of an annotated gene or gene product to another gene or group of genes.

entries.name: motif similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of match to sequence model evidence that is based on the presence of a recognized domain or motif in a gene product's (usually protein) primary sequence.

entries.name: match to InterPro member signature evidence
entries.description: A type of match to sequence model evidence resulting from a positive match of a protein, or set of proteins to a predictive model (signature) in the InterPro database.

entries.name: BLAST evidence used in manual assertion
entries.description: A type of BLAST evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

entries.name: protein BLAST evidence used in manual assertion
entries.description: A type of protein BLAST evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

entries.name: nucleotide BLAST evidence used in manual assertion
entries.description: A type of nucleotide BLAST evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

entries.name: author statement supported by traceable reference
entries.description: A type of author statement in which the author makes a statement that is not supported by information in that particular publication, but rather can be traced to a reference cited by that publication.

entries.name: author statement without traceable support
entries.description: A type of author statement that is not associated with results presented or a cited reference.

entries.name: no evidence data found
entries.description: A type of curator inference that results when research finds no evidence information in the scientific literature, at reference databases, or from other resources.

entries.name: obsolete not_recorded
entries.description: The evidence not_recorded appears in some legacy annotations; it should not be used for new annotations.

entries.name: transient rescue experiment evidence
entries.description: A type of functional complementation evidence resulting from the introduction of nucleic acids, which are not permanently incorporated into the genome, to temporarily prevent, or "rescue" an organism from a condition.

entries.name: obsolete protein assay evidence
entries.description: OBSOLETE. A type of direct assay evidence resulting from determining the presence, abundance, structure, function, or activity of proteins.

entries.name: immunological assay evidence
entries.description: A type of affinity evidence resulting from quantitation of the analyte which depends on the reaction of an antigen (analyte) and an antibody.

entries.name: similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of evidence resulting from comparing likeness of distinct biological entities.

entries.name: gain-of-function mutant phenotypic evidence
entries.description: A type of mutant phenotype evidence resulting from an altered gene product which possesses a new molecular function or a new pattern of gene expression.

entries.name: sequence similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of similarity based on biomolecular sequence.

entries.name: spatial pattern of protein expression evidence
entries.description: A type of protein expression evidence that accounts for the position of RNA translation to a protein product ranging in scale from differing tissues to regions of an anatomical structure.

entries.name: spatial pattern of transcript expression evidence
entries.description: A type of transcript expression evidence that accounts for the position of transcription ranging in scale from the position of genes on the chromosome to differing tissues to regions of an anatomical structure.

entries.name: reporter gene assay evidence
entries.description: A type of expression pattern evidence that is based on the expression pattern of a reporter gene.

entries.name: voucher specimen phenotypic analysis evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental phenotypic evidence based on an authors phenotypic description of a species (or higher-level group), which explicitly references an observation made of a voucher specimen (a specimen with a permanent museum catalog).

entries.name: genetic similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of similarity based on genotype without respect to expression.

entries.name: suppressor/enhancer interaction phenotypic evidence
entries.description: A type of genetic interaction evidence resulting from a second mutation that either suppresses or enhances the phenotype expressed from an original mutation.

entries.name: automatically integrated combinatorial evidence used in automatic assertion
entries.description: A type of automatically integrated combinatorial evidence that is used in an automatic assertion.

entries.name: double mutant phenotypic evidence
entries.description: A type of mutant phenotype evidence resulting from an experiment typically constructed to determine if two different genes have an observable genetic interaction (functional connection) as the result of a mutation occurring in the alleles of the two genes of interest.

entries.name: obsolete array experiment evidence
entries.description: This general 'array experiment' term should not be used - replace with the specific type of array (e.g. ECO:0000097, ECO:0000062, ECO:0000104, etc.)

entries.name: epistatic interaction phenotypic evidence
entries.description: A type of genetic interaction evidence resulting from the suppression of one allelic effect by an allele at another genetic locus.

entries.name: phenotypic similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of similarity based on the expression of a genotype in an environment.

entries.name: expression microarray evidence
entries.description: A type of transcript expression evidence resulting from simultaneous profiling of the expression levels of thousands of genes in a single experiment allowing analysis of genes and their networks.
synonyms: differential gene expression evidence from microarray experiment

entries.name: experimental phenotypic evidence
entries.description: A type of experimental evidence that is based on an observable characteristic trait, which is the result of the expression of an organisms genotype in an environment.

entries.name: positional similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic similarity evidence based on the similarity of stucture locations or arrangements.

entries.name: quantitative trait analysis evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic evidence that is based on a gene product that is associated with a quantitative trait locus, but has not been cloned.

entries.name: cRNA to DNA expression microarray evidence
entries.description: A type of expression microarray evidence where expression level is quantified by sample biotin-labeled cRNA (transcribed from an unknown RNA sample) hybridized to DNA oligonuclotides immoblized on a solid surface.

entries.name: compositional similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic similarity evidence based on the similarity of the histological makeup of structures.

entries.name: functional complementation in heterologous system evidence
entries.description: A type of functional complementation evidence that is based on the insertion of a wild-type copy of a gene into a heterologous organism, with the mutation occurring in a homologous gene.

entries.name: yeast one-hybrid evidence
entries.description: A type of bait-prey hybrid interaction evidence that is based on a protein-DNA complementation assay where a single promoter acts as bait and is screened against a library of prey transcription factors.

entries.name: developmental similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic similarity evidence based on the similarity of embryological or post-embryonic orgin of structures.

entries.name: yeast 2-hybrid evidence
entries.description: A type of bait-prey hybrid interaction evidence that is based on detection of protein-protein interaction by activation of a yeast reporter gene after a bait protein fused to a DNA-binding domain (which has been transfected into a yeast cell) is used to screen a cDNA library of clones fused to an activation domain.

entries.name: differential methylation hybridization evidence
entries.description: A type of in vitro methylation assay evidence where methylation-sensitive restriction enzymes are utilized to compare differential methylation between two samples by first shearing DNA, followed by end-blunting, ligation of linkers, methylation sensitive restriction, PCR using linker primers, dye labeling and relative quantification of methylated DNA fragments by two-colored array hybridization to a CpG island microarray for visual assessment.

entries.name: co-immunoprecipitation evidence
entries.description: A type of immunoprecipitation evidence that involves precipitating two or more proteins via binding to an antibody specific to a single protein, followed by protein identification.

entries.name: morphological similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic similarity evidence based on the similarity of shape, structure, or configuration in structures.

entries.name: obsolete experimental genomic evidence
entries.description: OBSOLETE. A type of experimental evidence that is based on the characterization of an attribute of the genome underlying a gene product.

entries.name: split-ubiquitin functional complementation evidence
entries.description: A type of protein fragment functional complementation evidence that is based on detection of protein-protein interaction between a bait and prey protein by in vivo reconstitution of split-ubiquitin (when bait and prey interact) and release of a reporter protein.

entries.name: gene expression similarity evidence
entries.description: A type of phenotypic similarity evidence that is based on the categorization of genes by the similarity of expression profiles.

entries.name: far-Western blotting evidence
entries.description: A type of physical interaction evidence that is based on detection of protein-protein interactions by separation of target proteins by SDS-PAGE which are blotted to a membrane, followed by denaturation and renaturation, probing with purified bait proteins, and detection of the target-bait complexes.

entries.name: methylation-specific polymerase chain reaction evidence
entries.description: A type of in vitro methylation assay evidence resulting from initial modification of DNA by sodium bisulfite, converting all unmethylated cytosines to uracil, and subsequent amplification with primers specific for methylated versus unmethylated DNA.

entries.name: southern hybridization evidence
entries.description: A type of DNA detection assay evidence that is based on detection of a specific DNA sequence by hybridization of labeled probes to any immobilized DNA fragment with sequence similarity.

entries.name: affinity chromatography evidence
entries.description: A type of affinity evidence that results from separation of biochemical mixtures by selective binding of a compound to an immobilized compound on a polymeric matrix, subsequent removal of unattached components, and then displacement of the bound compound.

entries.name: phylogenetic evidence
entries.description: A type of similarity that indicates common ancestry.

entries.name: targeting sequence prediction evidence
entries.description: A type of motif similarity evidence that is based on detection of a targeting sequence in the primary sequence of a protein through computational prediction and/or a manual examination of the sequence.

entries.name: obsolete polymerase chain reaction evidence
entries.description: OBSOLETE. A type of experimental genomic evidence where DNA polymerase is used to synthesize new strand of DNA complementary to the offered template strand.

entries.name: transmembrane domain prediction evidence
entries.description: A type of motif similarity evidence that is based on detection of one, or more, transmembrane domains in the primary sequence of a protein through computational prediction and/or a manual examination of the sequence.

entries.name: gene neighbors evidence
entries.description: A type of genomic context evidence in which a gene product's identity is supported on the basis of the identity of neighboring genes.

entries.name: immunoprecipitation evidence
entries.description: A type of protein binding evidence where an antibody specific for a target protein is used to precipitate the target protein along with any other molecules (e.g. protein, nucleic acid) that are bound to the target protein.

entries.name: amplification of intermethylated sites evidence
entries.description: A type of in vitro methylation assay evidence resulting from a genome-wide estimate of DNA methylation by differential enzymatic digestion of genomic DNA with methylation-sensitive and methylation-insensitive isoschizomers, followed by restrained PCR amplification of sequences methylated at both ends and resolving the PCR products in a denaturing polyacrylamide-sequencing gel to generate fingerprints that consist of multiple anonymous bands that represent the DNA methylome of the cell.

entries.name: immunolocalization evidence
entries.description: A type of microscopy evidence where antibodies are used to detect the location of molecules or other structures within cells or tissues.

entries.name: biological system reconstruction evidence
entries.description: A type of curator inference that is based on a combination of experimental evidences or existing models of that system in a related species used for the reconstruction of a biological system.

entries.name: restriction landmark genomic scanning evidence
entries.description: A type of DNA detection assay evidence resulting from the use of restriction enzymes for direct end-labeling of DNA (creating landmarks), followed by high-resolution two-dimensional electrophoresis to visualize the landmarks.

entries.name: immunogold labelling evidence
entries.description: A type of immunolocalization evidence where the antibodies are labeled with colloidal gold particles whose location is then detected via microscopy.

entries.name: epitope-tagged protein immunolocalization evidence
entries.description: A type of immunolocalization evidence in which recombinant proteins fused with epitopes are recognized by antibodies.

entries.name: array-based sequence capture evidence
entries.description: A type of DNA detection assay evidence evidence where gene sequence or variation is determined or detected by fluorescently-labeled DNA fragments hybridized to sequence-specific oligonucleotides immobilized on an array.

entries.name: obsolete biological assay evidence
entries.description: The children of 'biological assay evidence' have been moved under 'direct assay evidence', and this term has been deprecated with no children left.

entries.name: cell growth regulation assay evidence
entries.description: A type of cell growth assay evidence that measures one or more aspects of cell growth over a specified time period to indicate an induced or repressed regulatory effect.

entries.name: electrophoretic mobility shift assay evidence
entries.description: A type of affinity evidence based on an electrophoretic mobility shift of macromolecules, where proteins, nucleic acids, or both, are combined and the resulting mixtures are electrophoresed under native conditions through polyacrylamide or agarose gel to detect interactions/complexes between proteins and/or nucleic acid.

entries.name: cDNA to DNA expression microarray evidence
entries.description: A type of expression microarray evidence where expression level is quantified by fluroescently-labeled cDNA (reverse transcribed from an unknown RNA sample) hybridized to DNA immobilized on a solid surface.

entries.name: obsolete in situ hybridization evidence
entries.description: OBSOLETE. A type of nucleic acid hybridization evidence based on localization of a specific segment of DNA or RNA by the application of a complementary strand of nucleic acid to which a reporter molecule is attached.

entries.name: fractionation evidence
entries.description: A type of direct assay evidence resulting from the separation of various cell components while preserving their individual functions.

entries.name: Affymetrix GeneChip evidence
entries.description: A type of cRNA to DNA expression microarray evidence resulting from the use of a proprietary Affymetrix GeneChip System for analyzing complex genetic information.

entries.name: co-fractionation evidence
entries.description: A type of fractionation evidence resulting from a protein fractionated with other compounds, factors, or macromolecules.

entries.name: DNA to cDNA expression microarray evidence
entries.description: A type of expression microarray evidence where expression levels are quantified by hybridization of fluorescently-labeled DNA to cDNA (reverse transcribed from an unknown RNA sample) immobilized on a solid surface.

entries.name: Nimblegen array evidence
entries.description: A type of cDNA to DNA expression microarray evidence resulting from the use of a proprietary NimbleGen array to measure expression levels.

entries.name: northern blot evidence
entries.description: A type of transcript expression evidence based on electrophoresis and probing to determine levels of RNA expression using a complementary hybridization probe on the separated RNA samples.

entries.name: reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction evidence
entries.description: A type of RNA detection assay evidence where an RNA transcript is reverse transcribed into cDNA and amplified to qualitatively detect gene expression.
synonyms: reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction transcription evidence

entries.name: RNA protection assay evidence
entries.description: A type of nuclease protection assay evidence where mRNA is hybridized with radiolabeled RNA probes after which RNAse is added to digest the unbound, nonresistant single-stranded overhang regions, later the remaining probe target hybrids are purified and resolved on denaturing polyacrylamide gel, and quantified by autoradiography.
